sense.definition: a long-distance athletic event where competitors must maintain contact with the ground and keep their leading leg straight

examples.x: She trained daily to improve her technique in race walking.
Race walking requires athletes to keep one foot on the ground at all times.
He won the gold medal in the 20-kilometer race walking event.
The race walking competition took place on a scenic city route.
Race walking can be as physically demanding as running.

sense.definition: a type of race that takes place on natural outdoor surfaces such as fields, hills, and woods

examples.x: Cross-country running requires a lot of endurance because the course can be very challenging.
She joined the cross-country running team to improve her stamina and enjoy outdoor exercise.
The school organized a cross-country running event for students this weekend.
Cross-country running is a great way to explore nature while staying active.
Many people enjoy cross-country running because it combines physical activity with beautiful scenery.

sense.definition: a form of long-distance running that takes place on paved roads

examples.x: The marathoner focused on road running to prepare for the upcoming race.
His training regimen includes regular road running sessions to build endurance.
Road running allows you to cover longer distances without worrying about rough terrain.
The local running club organizes weekly road running events for its members.
Road running shoes are designed to provide cushioning and support for hard surfaces.

sense.definition: a running distance that is longer than a standard marathon

examples.x: His passion for ultra running drives him to continuously push his limits.
Ultra running often involves navigating varied terrain and weather conditions.
She set a new personal record in her latest ultra running race.
Many ultra running races take place in remote and challenging environments.
He completed an ultra running race through rugged mountain terrain.

sense.definition: a sport that involves using acrobatic movements to navigate obstacles and terrain, often in urban environments, with an emphasis on creativity and self-expression

examples.x: He spent hours practicing freerunning in the park, jumping off benches and flipping over rails.
She showed off her skills in freerunning by climbing up the wall and leaping to the next platform.
After watching a freerunning video, he was inspired to try jumping over fences and sliding under bars.

sense.definition: (running) the number of steps in every one minute

examples.x: The coach emphasized the importance of maintaining a consistent cadence throughout the race.
Her cadence gradually increased as she picked up speed during the final stretch.
I use a metronome to help me improve my running cadence and efficiency.
Running with a higher cadence can reduce the risk of injury by lessening the impact on joints.
She focused on matching her breathing pattern to her cadence to maximize performance.

sense.definition: (running) the way the foot makes contact with the ground during each stride

examples.x: With practice, he corrected his foot strike, leading to faster race times.
The athlete's foot strike became more fluid and natural after working with a running coach.
She analyzed her foot strike using video feedback to identify areas for improvement.

sense.definition: a sustained effort at a comfortably hard pace, typically faster than a runner's normal training pace

examples.x: His coach instructed him to maintain a steady tempo pace throughout the tempo run.
The tempo run felt challenging but rewarding, pushing her to her limits.
They scheduled their tempo run for the middle of the week to break up their training.
He gradually increased the intensity of his tempo run as he became fitter.
The tempo run helped her get accustomed to running at a faster pace for longer distances.

sense.definition: a sustained period of continuous running at a moderate to high intensity

examples.x: Many athletes prefer long-distance running as a form of stress relief.
Long-distance running events often attract participants from around the world.
Long-distance running is a great way to improve cardiovascular health.
Long-distance running can be challenging, but the sense of accomplishment is worth it.
His dedication to long-distance running earned him a spot on the national team.

sense.definition: a racing strategy in which the runner runs faster in the second half comparing to the first half of the race

examples.x: She ran a negative split in the marathon, finishing the second half faster than the first.
The experienced runner strategically planned for a negative split to conserve energy for the final stretch.
Running a negative split requires pacing oneself carefully in the early stages of the race.
He focused on maintaining a steady pace in the first half of the race to set up for a negative split.
Running a negative split is often considered a sign of effective race strategy and fitness.

sense.definition: a race where participants move swiftly on foot to reach a specific distance or finish line

examples.x: He won the 400-meter run at the track meet, setting a new school record.
He placed third in the 10K charity run, raising funds for cancer research.

sense.definition: the euphoric feeling experienced after a challenging run due to the release of endorphins
the running races ranging from 800 meters to 1,500 meters in length

examples.x: She's training specifically for middle-distance running.
Middle-distance running events often demand both speed and endurance.
She's known for her achievements in middle-distance running.
She's focusing on mental toughness to excel in middle-distance running.
His training regimen focuses on optimizing performance in middle-distance running.

sense.definition: a type of running in which one runs full speed in a short distance
to run for doing exercise or as a sport

examples.x: As part of his training routine, the athlete runs several miles each day.
She used to run regularly during her college years to stay in shape.
The club organizes weekly sessions where members can run together
